key_points.raw:
ABOUT [ edit ]

State Bank of India is a Fortune 500 company. It is an Indian Multinational, Public Sector banking and financial services statutory body headquartered in Mumbai. It is the largest and oldest bank in India with over 200 years of history.[1]

KEY POINTS [ edit ]

Market Share[1]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank has a deposit market share of about 22% and a net advance share of 20% in India.

Business Segments
Retail Banking: 36% in Q3 FY26 vs 35% in FY23
Corporate Banking: 21% in Q3 FY26 vs 21% in FY23
Treasury Operations: 18% in Q3 FY26 vs 22% in FY23
Insurance Business: 21% in Q3 FY26 vs 18% in FY23
Others: 5% in Q3 FY26 vs 4% in FY23 [2][3]

Key Ratios
NIM: 3.12% in Q3 FY26 vs 3.39% in FY23
CRAR: 14.04% in Q3 FY26 vs 14.68% in FY23
GNPA: 1.57% in Q3 FY26 vs 2.78% in FY23
NNPA: 0.39% in Q3 FY26 vs 0.67% in FY23
PCR: 75.54% in Q3 FY26 vs 76.39% in FY23
Slippage Ratio: 7% in Q3 FY26 vs 0.65% in FY23 [4][5]

Domestic Branch Network
Branches ('000): 23.13 in Q3 FY26 vs 22.41 in FY23
BC Outlets ('000): 79.6 in Q3 FY26 vs 76.1 in FY23
ATMs ('000): 63 in Q3 FY26 vs 65.6 in FY23 [6][7][8]

International Presence
The company operates 241 overseas offices across 29 countries, including Canada, Brazil, Russia, Germany, France, Turkey, etc. [9] The international business accounted for 15% of the total advances and 4% of the total deposits in Q3 FY26.[10][11]

Loan Book[10]
As of Q3 FY26, the bank has advances of Rs. 46,83,508 Cr vs Rs. 40,67,752 Cr in Q3 FY25.
Domestic Advance Mix:
Retail: 42%
Corporate: 33%
SME: 15%
Agri: 10%

Deposits[11]
As of Q3 FY26, the company has total deposits of Rs. 57,01,309 Cr vs Rs. 52,29,384 Cr in Q3 FY25.
Domestic Deposits Mix:
Term Deposits: 61%
CASA: 39%

Financial Inclusion[12]
The bank promotes financial inclusion through 79,630 Business Correspondent (BC) outlets, processing 3.27 Lac Cr transactions worth Rs. 58.68 Cr. Deposits in Financial Inclusion (FI) accounts increased to Rs. 82,350 Cr across 4,475 Cr accounts. The bank holds the largest market share among PSBs in social security schemes, with 46.83% in PMJJBY, 39.40% in PMSBY, and 32.14% in APY as of Q3 FY26.

Digital Initiatives[13][14]
The bank offers digital services via WhatsApp banking, digital loans, cross-selling products, and a payment ecosystem featuring QR codes, POS machines, and online credit facilities. Alternate channels like Internet, Mobile, UPI, and YONO account for 94% of its transactions, securing a 24.78% market share in UPI remittances. It processed 1,562 Cr UPI transactions, registered ~88 Lac YONO users, and disbursed 2.2 lakh digital loans in Q3 FY26.

Subsidiaries
1) SBI Life Insurance: Leadership in the private market with a 28.1% Individual New Business Premium market share.
2) SBI Card: No.2 player in Cards with 218 lakh cards in force and No.3 in Spends.[15]
3) SBI Fund: As of Q3 FY26, SBIFML has a quarterly Average AUM of ₹ 12.49 Lakh Cr with a market share of 15.42%.
4) SBI General: Ranked 7th amongst the Private players in 9MFY26 with a market share of 6.64%.
5) SBI Cap: Ranked No.1 for Debt Private placement of bonds in 9MFY26 with market share of ~35%.[16]

IPO of SBI Funds[17]
The bank’s Executive Committee has approved divestment of 6.30% stake (3.21 crore equity shares) in SBI Funds Management Ltd via an IPO, subject to regulatory approvals. The transaction is aimed at partial monetization of its asset management subsidiary while retaining majority control, and is expected to enhance transparency, unlock value, and support capital optimization.

Acquisition[18]
Bank’s Executive Committee has approved divestment of 6.30% stake (3.21 crore equity shares) in SBI Funds Management Ltd via an IPO, subject to regulatory approvals. The transaction is aimed at partial monetization of its asset management subsidiary while retaining majority control, and is expected to enhance transparency, unlock value, and support capital optimization.

Focus[19]
The bank expects a loan book growth of 13% to 15% in FY27 revised from 12% to 14%.
